text:
Once upon a time in an enchanting forest called Everwood, there lived a curious fox named Finn. Every morning Finn would trot over to the enchanted lake, its waters reflecting the dawn stars. One particularly serene day, while dipping his tails into the crystal-clear water, Finn heard a whispering stream.

The stream spoke of a magical creature, a wise old owl, who would grant three wishes every moon. Finn was captivated. On his first wish-seeking moon, he wished for a map showing the quickest way home.

In his second, he wished for a pet porcupine—someone to talk to during his nocturnal adventures. Three gentle porcupines appeared instead, teaching him the depths of the woodland mysteries.

In his third wish, Finn declared a wish forest around him so big that it couldn't bear fruit, just endless wisdom. An elder fox appeared, his voice as ancient as the oak trees. The elder said, "Understanding is your treasure."

Finn never needed a map
